title: estimate_tokens uses magic constants *2, /3, *13/10 with no derivation or test pin
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
naming
## Location
src/compaction.rs:208-230 (estimate_tokens)
## Violation
The estimator mixes three magic numbers with no symbolic name or rationale:

```rust
let char_estimate = char_count / 4;
let word_estimate = word_count * 13 / 10;
let base_estimate = (char_estimate * 2 + word_estimate) / 3;
let non_ascii_adjustment = non_ascii_count / 2;
base_estimate + non_ascii_adjustment
```

`/ 4`, `* 13 / 10` (= 1.3 tokens per word), `* 2 + ... / 3` (weighted average biased toward char count), and `/ 2` (half of non-ASCII count) are all unjustified. The accuracy gap relative to a real tokenizer is the proximate cause of #321 (compaction firing late on CJK input). The doc comment says "approximate" but does not cite a corpus or tolerance.

Two specific concerns:
1. Future maintainers cannot tell which constant to nudge when accuracy needs to improve — `4` vs `1.3` vs `1/2` are all related guesses with no derivation.
2. No property test asserts the estimate is within ±N% of a known-truth tokenizer for the calibration corpus, so adjustments will be flying blind.
## Mandated refactor
1. Promote the constants to named, doc-commented consts: `CHAR_TO_TOKEN_DIVISOR`, `WORD_TO_TOKEN_NUMERATOR/DENOMINATOR`, `NON_ASCII_TOKEN_BOOST_DIVISOR`, `CHAR_WEIGHT_NUMERATOR/DENOMINATOR`.
2. Cite the source (anthropic-tokenizer empirical run, etc.) in the doc comment.
3. Add a property test fixture (`tests/fixtures/tokenizer_corpus.json`) with text→ground-truth-token-count pairs and assert estimate is within ±20%.
4. Long-term tracked by #321 (tiktoken integration); this issue is the cleanup that makes #321's calibration safer.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
